final_answer:
Yes, Phase 3 is implemented.

Why this matters: validator-set updates are now stake-ranked and capped, so active-validator membership follows deterministic top-stake selection instead of uncapped “all-current” inclusion, which matches the target BSC-style behavior.

I changed [validator_set.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/validator_set.go:26) to add `ActiveValidatorSetCap = 21`, replaced `GetUpdatedValidators` with ranked/capped selection logic in [validator_set.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/validator_set.go:619), and added one shared helper (`selectStakeRankedActiveValidators`) in [validator_set.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/validator_set.go:659) to avoid duplicate logic. I also added focused tests for cap behavior, boundary swap, and deterministic tie-breaks in [validator_set_selection_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/validator_set_selection_test.go:1).

Checks run (all pass):
`go test ./x/stake/... -count=1`
`go test ./bridge/... -count=1`
(and earlier full module gate in this branch: `x/stake`, `x/chainmanager`, `x/clerk`, `bridge` all passed)

What should be done next:
1. If you want cap configurability instead of fixed `21`, move `ActiveValidatorSetCap` into module params and wire governance updates.
2. Proceed to the next phase and keep the same pass/fail test gate after each phase.
